macro_summary: Thursday saw a violent reversal as President Trump signalled an Iran peace deal was close, reversing the earlier selloff on Hormuz closure and 4.2% CPI. The S&P 500 surged 2.1%, the Nasdaq 4.0%, and the Dow jumped 900 points. WTI ended down 5.5% to $86.73 despite an early spike above $92, as peace hopes overwhelmed supply-shock fears. The VIX plunged 12.5% to 19.44, while gold rose 2.8% and the 10-year yield eased to 4.46%. The ECB raised rates as expected, adding to global tightening, but the risk-on mood was driven by ceasefire bets.
commodities_note: WTI settled at $86.73, down 5.5%, erasing the intraday Hormuz-driven spike as peace hopes sent crude tumbling. Gold rallied 2.8% to $4,209, reflecting lingering safe-haven demand despite the equity surge. The US 10-year yield dipped to 4.46%. The US Dollar Index was flat at 99.82.

Cluster relative strength: outperforming Singapore peers by 0.6% today.

thesis_highlights: Blackwell Ultra ramp + Vera Rubin sampling are the multi-leg bull thesis — no confirmed revenue from Vera Rubin yet.

NVDA fiscal year offset from CY: Q4 FY2026 print = $68.1B (+73%), Q1 FY2027 guide = $78B (Feb 26 2026 earnings call). Vera Rubin samples shipping but contribute no booked revenue — Blackwell + Hopper still drive the print.

writeup.headline: Downgraded to WATCH as R:R compressed to 0.7:1 after the bounce.
writeup.what_to_do: NVDA rebounded 2.9% but remains just below its 50-day (SMA50 $206.11). The risk/reward has shrunk to 0.7:1, with support at $194.51 (5.1% down) and resistance at $211.92 (3.4% up) — yesterday's 2:1 setup is gone. Volume was below average, so conviction in the move is low. Reassess on a reclaim of the 50-day with above-average volume, or a pullback to $194.51 support. Blackwell Ultra and Vera Rubin sampling remain multi-quarter catalysts. Fragility: NVDA's thesis rests on continued hyperscaler capex — any softening in AI spending would break both legs. NVDA CAUTION has historically avoided relative weakness in 18/23 cases (10d window).
writeup.valuation_judgment: Trades at a 45% discount to the semis cluster median — the cheapest in the group on forward PE.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if NVDA fails to reclaim SMA50 ($206.11) on above-average volume within the next 10 sessions.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks remain all WATCH, with no changes from yesterday. US10Y at 4.46% is still stubbornly above the 4.20% threshold, and the MAS S$NEER stance keeps NIMs compressed from both sides. The structural headwind is intact despite the global risk-on move.
singapore.key_development: A UOB Kay Hian report flagged that DBS is most exposed to INR and IDR weakness, adding a currency-translation layer to the rate-driven NIM pressure.

semiconductors.summary: Semis surged across the board, led by AMD (+10.9%), MU (+13.5%), and INTC (+11.3%), as Trump's peace hint crushed oil and lifted growth. Still, 11 of 17 names remain hard-blocked on extreme extension, leaving only a few near-50-day setups (NVDA, AVGO, LITE) potentially actionable. The SOXX’s +9.7% daily move is a relief rally, not yet a structural reset.
semiconductors.key_development: BofA raised AMD's target to $560 and double-upgraded Intel, adding a fundamental catalyst to the price surge — but neither name cleared its extension block.

big_tech.summary: BigTech diverged: MSFT slipped again, making it the most oversold name in the cluster with the best R:R (2.3:1), while GOOG and AMZN bounced modestly but offered no entry. The cluster's defining tension remains MSFT's OpenAI concentration risk versus GOOG's TPU edge.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — oversold MSFT vs. GOOG premium
big_tech.key_development: Alphabet's $80B debt raise for AI (per Motley Fool) adds to the capex arms race, but the market rewarded GOOG only a muted 1.3% — the spending trajectory is known, not new.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Paired with LITE as NVDA's $2B photonics investment partner; AI datacenter optical interconnect buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE reclaims its 50-day on above-average volume, confirming the optics bounce is not a head-fake.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC challenger to AVGO and NVDA, directly benefiting from hyperscaler capex shift to custom silicon.
interconnected.entry_note: Track MRVL if AVGO's AVGO earnings on July 24 show custom-silicon revenue acceleration.

interconnected.name: Qualcomm
interconnected.reason: Snapdragon AI edge chips leverage the on-device inference trend; the AI PC cycle maps to AMD's Ryzen AI ramp.
interconnected.entry_note: Enter watchlist if AMD's Ryzen AI 400 series OEM shipments in Q2 2026 exceed 10M units within the first quarter.

active_risks: US–Iran Hormuz blockade: Iran closed the Strait, US launched strikes, then Trump signalled a peace deal was close — oil swung violently.
ECB rate hike and global tightening: ECB raised rates to 2.75% to fight war-induced inflation; BOJ hike next week could add to dollar weakness.
US-China tech blacklist expands: Pentagon reaffirms Alibaba, BYD, Baidu, Unitree on military list; rare earth exports remain restricted.
Russia–Ukraine drone war: Ukraine's 'highway of death' campaign menaces Russian logistics; second-order food supply risk persists.
geopolitical.market_impact: The peace-deal hope unleashed a massive risk-on rotation: SPY +2.1%, semis +10%, oil -5.5%, VIX -12.5%. If peace materialises, oil could fall to $75–80, removing the inflation tailwind and boosting tech multiples further. Singapore banks and BigTech would benefit from lower US rates.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump announced a ceasefire proposal and said a deal with Iran was close; markets surged on the news. Iran's earlier Hormuz closure and US strikes were quickly overshadowed.

base.description: Ceasefire holds tentatively, oil stabilises near $85–90, VIX stays below 22; semis remain elevated but consolidate. NVDA and MSFT offer selective entries as tech re-rates lower rates.

optimistic.description: Full peace deal signed, Hormuz reopens, WTI drops to $70s; rate hike fears vanish, 10Y falls below 4.2%. BigTech and Singapore banks surge — DBS and OCBC become actionable as NIM relief triggers.

pessimistic.description: Trump's deal collapses, US restrikes Iran, Hormuz remains closed; oil spikes above $100, VIX >25, and semis crash. All CAUTION tickers remain blocked, and AVGO/LITE suffer as capex fears return.

wildcard.description: SpaceX IPO overwhelms market, sparking a liquidity drain from tech; SPY drops despite good macro as capital rotates to the new listing. TSMC and ASML see short-term selling on positioning.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold current positions; do not chase the peace rally. Wait for the FOMC on June 18 to confirm rate trajectory before deploying fresh capital.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously bullish post-peace rally, but extreme extension keeps entries scarce
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 11 of 27 names are hard-blocked on technical extension despite the surge, leaving selective opportunities only in oversold BigTech (MSFT) and near-50-day semis (NVDA, LITE). The FOMC next week is the next major binary.

scheduled_tech_events.notes: Vera Rubin samples confirmed shipping; optics integration revealed in Rubin rack design. Blackwell Ultra → Rubin transition timeline given.
Taiwan AI infrastructure partnerships; CoWoS capacity commitments from TSMC; HBM4 roadmap. Direct thesis-confirming event for AI Power Infrastructure cluster.
AI kit, on-device model integration, Vision Pro 2 rumours. Competitive signal for MSFT Copilot and GOOG Gemini on-device.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: No hike; dot plot holds or turns more dovish. Equity markets rally, 10Y falls.

O39_SI → BUY candidate (NIM relief starts to price in)
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Hawkish hold or explicit rate hike signal. Selloff in tech, spike in yields.
bearish_impact: NVDA → deeper CAUTION (semis sell on higher rates)
SG banks → all remain CAUTION (US10Y pushes further above 4.20%)

macro_context_line: Rate backdrop (FRED, not live): payrolls +172k (eased from +179k); unemployment 4.3% (flat); last CPI 4.3% YoY (up). Reconcile the rate lean in the macro_summary against this when a CPI/FOMC/jobs event is near.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=4) / ACCUMULATE (n=11) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
